summary: The cross-chain landscape has split into two layers: generalized messaging protocols (LayerZero, Wormhole, Chainlink CCIP, Axelar, Hyperlane) that secure and route arbitrary data/value, and value-transfer rails that increasingly run on top of them (Stargate, Circle CCTP, intent bridges like Across). As of mid-2026, LayerZero is the messaging volume leader (reported ~35% share of cross-chain messaging volume, 150M+ messages and $100B+ value transferred cumulatively; it acquired Stargate in 2025 for ~$110M to capture the value-transfer layer). Wormhole (~$41B+ total value transferred, deep DeFi/Solana integrations) and Chainlink CCIP (institutional focus; CCIP moved ~$18B in a single month by March 2026 vs $7.77B for all of 2025, riding SWIFT/bank integrations) are the other heavyweights. Axelar (~18% share, ~$353M monthly volume) saw Circle acquire developer Interop Labs in Dec 2025 (excluding the AXL token, which sold off ~18%). Hyperlane is the permissionless breadth leader (140+ chains). On the pure value-transfer side, native rollup/canonical bridges still hold the most locked value (Arbitrum, Base/Coinbase, Optimism canonical bridges each multi-billion TVL), while intent-based bridging (Across) and native USDC routing (Circle CCTP) dominate fast user transfers. The dominant structural trend is intents/solvers replacing classic lock-and-mint bridges: Across pioneered intent bridging in 2022 and ERC-7683 (ratified early 2025, co-authored by Across and Uniswap) is now the cross-chain order standard adopted across Across, UniswapX, CoW Protocol, Eco and deBridge DLN.

leaders.name: LayerZero (incl. Stargate)
leaders.chain: Omnichain — 130+ chains (Ethereum, all major rollups, Solana, BNB, etc.)
leaders.metric: Messaging market share / cumulative value transferred / messages
leaders.value: ~35% reported cross-chain messaging volume share; ~$684M monthly cross-chain volume; 150M+ messages and $100B+ value transferred cumulatively; 500+ apps
leaders.note: Current messaging-volume leader. V2 is the transport layer for Stargate ($70B+ cumulative cross-chain transactions), which LayerZero acquired in 2025 for ~$110M to own the value-transfer layer. ZRO token. Figures are protocol/secondary-source reported, not exchange-audited.

leaders.name: Wormhole
leaders.value: ~$41B+ total value transferred cumulatively; 19 Guardian nodes; integrated with Uniswap, Lido, Pyth, Circle CCTP across 8+ chains
leaders.note: Recovered from the Feb 2022 $320M+ exploit (120k wETH) to become a top messaging + token-bridge protocol, especially for Solana. W token (~$0.03, ~$177M mcap as of late 2025/2026). Powers native USDC transfers via CCTP integration.

leaders.name: Chainlink CCIP
leaders.chain: 70+ networks (Ethereum, major L2s, plus private/bank chains)
leaders.value: ~$18B moved in a single month by March 2026, up from $7.77B for all of 2025 (~1,972% YoY); connects 70+ networks
leaders.note: Fastest-growing on institutional/TradFi rails. Tied to SWIFT (11,500+ banks enabling on-chain wallet attachment Nov 2025) and framed by Standard Chartered as a bridge to the $150T SWIFT market. Oracle-grade verification via Chainlink DON; LINK token.

leaders.name: Axelar
leaders.chain: 70+ chains (Ethereum, major rollups, Solana, Sui, Aptos, Stellar, Cosmos/IBC)
leaders.value: ~18% reported messaging share; ~$353M monthly cross-chain volume; Q4 2025 daily tx +~140% vs Q2 2025
leaders.note: Validator-consensus (PoS) security, Interchain Token Service + ITS Hub (Jan 2025). Circle agreed to acquire developer Interop Labs (Dec 15, 2025) for institutional readiness — but excluded the AXL token, which dropped ~18% on the news. Historically led messaging volume in early 2025 per Binance Research before LayerZero pulled ahead.

leaders.name: Hyperlane
leaders.chain: 140+ chains (largest footprint; added TRON to reach 150+ in 2026)
leaders.value: 140+ chains connected — the largest of any messaging protocol; HYPER token launched April 2025
leaders.note: Differentiates on permissionless deployment: any chain can self-deploy contracts and connect without foundation/governance approval. Warp Routes for token bridging. Breadth leader rather than raw-volume leader.

leaders.name: Across
leaders.value: $28B+ cumulative bridged volume, 0 exploits; daily volume routinely >$50M; ERC-7683 orders ~88% of Across volume after Q3 2025 solver migration
leaders.note: Pioneered intent-based bridging (2022); relayers front destination funds in seconds and settle via UMA Optimistic Oracle. Co-author of ERC-7683 with Uniswap. The flagship of the intents/solvers wave; clears most user transfers in 2-30s. ACX token.

leaders.name: Circle CCTP (native USDC)
leaders.chain: 8+ major chains (Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base, OP, Avalanche, Solana, etc.)
leaders.value: Burn-and-mint native USDC routing used as the default stablecoin rail by aggregators (LI.FI, Squid, Jumper, deBridge) and bridges (Wormhole, Across)
leaders.note: Not a token-locking bridge but a canonical-issuer transfer protocol; eliminates wrapped-USDC fragmentation. Increasingly the backbone for cross-chain stablecoin transfer (USDC); strategically central given Circle's Axelar/Interop Labs acquisition.

leaders.name: Native rollup / canonical bridges (Arbitrum, Base, Optimism)
leaders.value: Arbitrum One canonical ~multi-billion (ecosystem TVL ~$16.9B May 2026 per secondary source); Base/Coinbase Bridge ~$5.8B TVL; Optimism Bridge ~$6.13B (Jan 2024 reference)
leaders.note: Hold the largest locked value of any 'bridge' because they are the L1<->L2 deposit contracts inheriting Ethereum security (~7-day withdrawal). Not in the messaging-volume race but dominate value-locked rankings. Figures are secondary-source / point-in-time and should be re-verified on DefiLlama.

leadership_shift: 2021 (lock-and-mint era): Value-locked bridges dominated — Multichain/Anyswap (~$6.5B across chains, $3.5B Fantom<->Ethereum), Binance Bridge (~$4.5B), Solana Wormhole (~$3.8B), plus Polygon PoS and Ronin. These were custody-heavy and became prime hack targets. 2022 (the bridge-hack crisis): >80% of the ~$1.2B+ hacked in 2022 flowed through bridges — Ronin ($600M+), Wormhole ($320M+, Feb 2022), Nomad, Harmony Horizon. Trust assumptions came under scrutiny and 'bridge wars' shifted toward security narratives. 2023 (collapse + messaging pivot): Multichain imploded after its CEO was detained in China (May 2023) and a $125M+ exploit hit in July 2023; it ceased operations, dragging Fantom TVL down ~67-80%. Confidence in monolithic lock-and-mint bridges broke; the market pivoted to generalized messaging (LayerZero, Wormhole rebuild, Axelar) and to native rollup bridges + Circle CCTP for value transfer. Early 2025 Axelar reportedly led messaging volume (Binance Research: Axelar ~2x Wormhole, ~8x CCIP on 30-day volume). Now (2025-2026): LayerZero leads messaging share (~35%), having acquired Stargate to own value transfer; Chainlink CCIP is the breakout institutional rail (monthly volume from ~$0.6B avg in 2025 to ~$18B by Mar 2026 via SWIFT/bank integrations); Wormhole holds strong DeFi/Solana share; Axelar slipped to ~18% and was absorbed by Circle (team/IP, not token); Hyperlane won breadth (140+ chains). Structurally, intents/solvers (Across, UniswapX, CoW, Eco, deBridge DLN) and the ERC-7683 standard (ratified 2025) are displacing classic bridges for user transfers, while native rollup bridges + CCTP hold the most locked value.
